headlines.title: Meet SK Hynix, the trillion-dollar South Korean chipmaker debuting on U.S. markets
headlines.summary: Following a more than sevenfold rally in its stock price over the past year, South Korea's SK Hynix is listing on the Nasdaq.

headlines.title: Stablecoin issuer Circle just got the greenlight to operate as a bank. The shares are up 12%
headlines.summary: Stablecoin issuer Circle surged in premarket trading after the U.S. Office of the Comptroller of the Currency granted it approval to operate as a trust bank.

headlines.title: Delta expects higher airfare to last, bringing 2026 profit goal in reach, CEO says
headlines.summary: Delta is the first of the U.S. airlines to report second-quarter results.

headlines.title: Meta found to breach EU laws with 'addictive' Instagram, Facebook designs
headlines.summary: Instagram and Facebook's "addictive" designs have put Meta in breach of the European Union's digital laws, the EU concluded Friday in a preliminary report.

headlines.title: AI, data center fears could be key in closely watched Michigan Democratic Senate primary
headlines.summary: Abdul El-Sayed and Rep. Haley Stevens will face off in the Michigan Democratic Senate primary on Aug. 4.

headlines.title: World oil demand set for first annual decline since 2020, IEA says
headlines.summary: Renewed escalation in the U.S.-Iran war could complicate matters and further cloud the outlook, the IEA warned Friday.

headlines.title: Kalshi traders see roughly 50% odds of a rate hike in 2026 as Fed is split on policy
headlines.summary: Wednesday's Fed minutes showed a divided outlook on where interest rates are headed this year. Traders on Kalshi see a 54% likelihood of a hike before 2027.

headlines.title: Clean power comeback? Don't count out renewable energy and this one stock in particular
headlines.summary: Clean power is responsible for roughly 90% of all new electrical capacity being added to the grid, according to the CEO of the American Clean Power Association.

headlines.title: Michael Burry bets on sportsbooks DraftKings and Flutter, sees prediction markets curbed by regulation
headlines.summary: The investor believes regulators will eventually crack down on prediction markets after competition from the upstarts pressured the stocks of sportsbooks.

headlines.title: Iran conflict flare-up awakens bears in airline stocks, but there's one big catch
headlines.summary: The U.S. Global Jets ETF is down 4% Wednesday, extending a two-day decline after challenging all-time highs last week.

headlines.title: Rivian tailspin hasn't shaken one trader's resolve
headlines.summary: Mike Khouw revisits his Rivian trade after the stock tanks on a share offering.

headlines.title: Kalshi traders see slim odds U.S. government will take a stake in OpenAI this year
headlines.summary: Traders on the prediction markets platform see a less than 30% likelihood the federal government will take a stake in AI companies Anthropic and OpenAI.

headlines.title: Kevin Warsh wants fewer press conferences. Why that’s bad for the economy and your money.
headlines.summary: The Fed chair is testifying before Congress this week. Lawmakers should ask him about transparency.

headlines.title: ‘I’ve plenty of time on my hands’: Advisers bombard me with offers of free steak dinners. Is it wrong to go for the food?
headlines.summary: “I get hungry for a meal at a good restaurant.”

headlines.title: Landon Donovan says there’s ‘zero chance’ he could’ve afforded to play youth soccer in America if he was a kid today
headlines.summary: The U.S. men’s soccer team once again didn’t make it past the Round of 16 in the World Cup — and some say the high cost to play youth soccer in America could be a key reason.

headlines.title: Delta absorbs highest fuel costs in its history, but also books record revenue
headlines.summary: Delta Air Lines’ profit dropped as the air carrier absorbed the highest quarterly fuel expense in its history.

headlines.title: The bar is high for S&P 500 earnings, but these as-yet-unrewarded sectors are where real surprises could be hiding
headlines.summary: Expectations for S&P 500 companies that will begin reporting next week are flying pretty  high, but only for a select few, notes HSBC.

headlines.title: ‘I’d hate to end up with an unexpected tax bill’: I’m 73 and still work full time. Can I avoid paying taxes on my Social Security benefits?
headlines.summary: “I’m actually earning more each week than I ever have before.”

observations: High concentration in Communication Services, primarily driven by META and NFLX.
Underexposure to preferred themes such as AI / compute infrastructure, small modular reactors / clean energy, and cybersecurity.
Cash position at 9.16% exceeds the minimum cash buffer requirement of 5%, but could be better deployed.
suggested_changes: Trim META by ~6 share(s) to bring weight under 25%
Consider reallocating cash to enhance exposure in preferred themes like AI or clean energy.
Evaluate positions in NFLX and OKLO for potential trimming or exit due to underperformance.
open_questions: What is the target allocation for preferred themes in the portfolio?
Are there any specific entry points or setups identified for potential new investments in preferred sectors?
What is the strategy for managing underperforming positions like OKLO and NFLX?

candidates.thesis: Advanced Micro Devices is a leader in semiconductor technology, benefiting from secular growth in AI and compute infrastructure. The stock is trading near its 52-week high but has a reasonable RSI of 55.8, indicating room for upside. Strong fundamentals and alignment with the AI theme make this a high-conviction pick.

Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing is the backbone of global semiconductor production, critical for AI and compute infrastructure. Trading at a healthy RSI of 50.5 and near its SMA50, it offers an attractive entry point for long-term growth.

Palo Alto Networks is a leader in cybersecurity, a secular growth theme with increasing demand as digital threats evolve. RSI is elevated at 64, but the stock has strong momentum and is trading well above its SMA50 and SMA200, indicating robust technicals.

ASML is the dominant supplier of lithography machines essential for semiconductor manufacturing, directly benefiting from the growth in AI and compute infrastructure. RSI is neutral at 51.6, and the stock is trading above its SMA50 and SMA200, suggesting strong technical support.

GEV is positioned to benefit from the clean energy transition, particularly in small modular reactors and advanced energy solutions. RSI is neutral at 52, and the stock is trading near its SMA50, offering a balanced entry point for long-term growth.
